safety: All BallPark facilities carry risks for players and spectators. Flying objects including balls and bats are some of those inherent concerns. We recommend all spectators sit behind our large backstop screens and observation decks and stay aware at all times.
All teams are required to have team insurance and all visitors must sign our park waiver.
If any injuries occur, immediately notify the umpire and the site director. An injury report must be filled out. If an ambulance is needed, we will help facilitate that as soon as possible.
Children should remain supervised by their parent or custodian at all times.
rules: No seeds, gum, or tobacco products. Seeds specifically harm the turf, and players may be removed along with their coach due to the seriousness of this damage.
No metal cleats, poles, or anything that can damage the turf. Lawn chairs are allowed but must be kept outside of the turf.
No outside food or drink. Teams may bring in ONE team cooler. Visitors may bring a small carry thermos/cooler, but it is subject to search. No outside alcohol.
No littering. Please use the garbage cans throughout the facility and help keep bathrooms, seating areas, and fields clean.
No fighting, cursing, stalking, or poor behavior. This is a family facility and adults are expected to act as role models.
insurance: Each team is required to have proof of insurance with them at all times. Proof must also be provided to Adventure Sports and Entertainment prior to the tournament. The insurance certificate must have no less than $2,000,000 General Aggregate and $1,000,000 for Each Occurrence. The insurance certificate must have “Adventure Sports and Entertainment” listed as the certificate holder and additionally insured.
umpires: Any foul language, arguing, berating, or unbecoming actions towards any umpire can result in either temporary expulsion or permanent banning from the facility.
equipment: Teams must bring their own practice balls for the batting cage and warm-up. Teams should also bring helmets, bats, gloves, catcher’s gear, first aid, and other equipment needed for the team. No metal cleats.
refundSummary: 45 days or more before an event: full refund if the event is not at max registration.
If the event has reached max registration, no refund will be given unless a replacement team is found.
Once schedules are set to be released on the Monday before the event, there will be no refunds regardless of whether a replacement can be found.
Less than 30 days before the event with a replacement team found prior to 48 hours before the event: 50% refund.
Within 96 hours of the event: no refund regardless of replacement team status.
Weather/event cancellation credits: 75% credit if no games are started, 50% credit if 25% of guaranteed games are started, 25% credit if 50% are started, and no credit if more than 50% are started.
refundDetails:
Adventure Sports understands that unforeseen circumstances, especially injuries, roster defections, player availability, etc. can occur over the course of a season. Teams should plan for the unexpected and understand that events sell out or reach max registration weeks and months before an event, forcing us to turn teams away. Also, the scheduling process starts several weeks before an event is scheduled to take place.

When a team withdraws prior to an event, the following conditions apply if the event is not currently at max registration. If the event has reached max registration, no refund will be given unless a replacement team is found. However, once schedules are set to be released on the Monday before the event there will be no refunds given regardless of whether or not a replacement can be found.

If a team withdraws 45 days or more before an event, a full refund will be provided.

If a team withdraws less than 30 days prior to the event and a replacement team can be found prior to 48 hours before the start of the event, a 50% refund shall be given.

No refund shall be given if a team withdraws within 45 days of the event and a replacement team cannot be found.

If a team withdraws within 96 hours of the event, no refund is given regardless if a replacement team would be found. It is the responsibility of the team to notify the tournament director that they are not able to participate.

Refunds for cancellations or shortening of an event:
While Adventure Sports and our host partners will make every attempt to play each event, weather and other unforeseen circumstances may prevent an event from being completed or in some cases, even starting. There is a great deal of preparation and work that goes into planning and preparing for tournaments including labor, insurance, and product purchases.

For all events:
A 75% credit will be given to teams that do not start any game in an event.
A 50% credit will be given to teams starting 25% of its guaranteed games.
A 25% credit will be given to teams starting 50% of its guaranteed games.
No credits will be given to teams starting more than 50% of its scheduled games.

All field rental costs, umpire fees, UIC fees, assigning fees, credit card fees, field labor, admin, and insurance must all be paid despite weather cancellations and in most cases team withdrawals. Program directors must fully pay the rental fee in advance, and with no refunds.

Note:
If governmental agencies shut down our ability to play due to a national emergency or a state emergency, refunds are not guaranteed and contingencies and actions will be determined at that time once we know the extent of any shutdown and how the extraordinary situation would impact our ability to remain in business.

In the case where teams are due a refund but are registered and unpaid for future events run by the same director, that refund shall be applied to the balance due on those future events.

Adventure Sports reserves the right to adjust this policy at any time individually to teams or as a whole.
rulesIntro: We are excited to host and rent our venue out to reputable directors. They have their own rules and policies and will be the organizer and operator. If you are at Adventure Sports Complex, please read and follow our facility rules.
Adventure Sports & Entertainment is a community resource and all-turf facility. We provide local programs and teams with many resources and rent our facility out for major and local events.

We are involved in many charitable efforts and need everyone’s cooperation to have a great experience and to protect the park for years to come. It is a major task to manage 2,000 visitors a day, especially with volunteers and limited resources.

Turf is expensive and valuable. Expect to continue to play through or around any issue as long as safety is not a concern. Weather updates will be posted on the Adventure Sports & Entertainment Facebook page as soon as possible.

We provide batting cages, observation decks, canopies for shade, house tents, seating, a clubhouse restaurant, video streaming, free cornhole/games, covered dugouts, lights, and more. We ask everyone to help keep the complex clean and enjoyable.
Adventure Sports reserves the right to expel and/or ban visitors for violating rules. Please help make every event a great experience.
rosters: The roster size can be unlimited for the number of players on the team. There can be no more than 3 complimentary admitted coaches allowed in the tournament. A team can add to the roster up to the first game. If an unforeseeable circumstance happens during tournament play where a coach needs to add to the roster, the coach must immediately submit reasoning to the tournament committee, and the committee will grant or deny the request. A player can be on the roster for two different age groups, but not on multiple teams within a single age group. If a player’s name shows up on multiple rosters, they must designate which team they are officially on. If a player is not on the roster and is playing in a game, that player will be immediately ejected from both the current game and the rest of the tournament. The current game will not revert back. Play will continue as it was at the point of ejection. You must submit a roster in order to play your first game.
